notes: This file records baseline scores for the repository against Requirement.md.
01_mathematics retains its original baseline score here; its post-revision score is recorded separately in updated_score.json.
Scores for non-mathematics files are approximate baseline evaluations intended for prioritization.

01_mathematics.summary: Original baseline retained here. Before revision, the file was strong but still partial because textbook guidance was not fully level-aware and the literature map was uneven across major subfields.

02_physics.summary: Very strong on curriculum, graduate depth, tools, and subfield realism; literature is broad but still somewhat uneven and partly survey-like rather than a fully curated research-entry canon.

03_chemistry.summary: Comprehensive from undergraduate labs to PhD specialization with strong instrument and textbook coverage; canonical literature is better than most files but still mixes exact landmarks with some generalized pointers.

04_biology.summary: One of the strongest files, with detailed curriculum, modern methods, and realistic subfield depth; it still needs more consistently curated course-level and subfield-specific literature to be fully research-ready.

05_earth_environmental_sciences.summary: Strong field realism with methods, data sources, and good disciplinary spread; textbook and literature guidance are solid but less systematically curated than the best files.

06_astronomy.summary: Good coverage of training path, observatories, and active frontiers with credible texts and papers; the literature remains somewhat survey-heavy and only partially structured as a research-entry roadmap.

07_mechanical_engineering.summary: Realistic engineering curriculum, tools, and methods are strong; literature quality drops because several entries are placeholder-style rather than tightly specified seminal works.

08_electrical_computer_engineering.summary: Solid breadth across core ECE domains with credible tools and textbook coverage; the literature is more representative than fully curated and the research-entry map is still partial.

09_civil_environmental_engineering.summary: Good professional realism, software specificity, and practical subfields; literature mixes exact landmarks with generalized references and graduate depth is lighter than the requirement asks.

10_chemical_biomolecular_engineering.summary: Usable core structure and plausible specialization areas, but methods, graduate depth, and canonical literature are substantially thinner than needed for a strong research roadmap.

11_biomedical_engineering.summary: Good disciplinary fit and plausible texts and methods, yet the file is compact enough that infrastructure, curriculum depth, and literature remain materially incomplete.

12_aerospace_engineering.summary: Clear scaffolding for curriculum and specialization, but research methods, textbook stratification, and landmark literature are too compressed for research-readiness.

13_materials_science_engineering.summary: Reasonably strong on methods, tools, and field-specific subareas; literature and course-by-course text coverage are present but not deep enough for a full research-entry roadmap.

14_industrial_systems_engineering.summary: Good operations and optimization orientation with realistic tools; literature and graduate depth are adequate but still too compact and generic for the target standard.

15_other_engineering.summary: Structurally weakest because it compresses several disciplines into one partial file; multiple subfields lack full methods, textbook, and literature treatment required by Requirement.md.

16_computer_science.summary: Strong curriculum and tool coverage with recognizable core texts, but the paper list contains several vague or placeholder-like entries and does not yet function as a fully curated research canon.

17_data_science.summary: Good practical framing and methods-tool realism, but textbook stratification and canonical literature are still too thin for a field spanning undergraduate training to research depth.

18_cybersecurity.summary: Practical tools and frontier topics are credible, but the literature and graduate-research structure remain somewhat template-like rather than fully concrete and field-defining.

19_medicine.summary: Strong professional realism, sensible credential path, and concrete landmark studies and guidelines; level-aware text curation and full research-training structure are still somewhat compressed.

20_biomedical_sciences.summary: Good biomedical research orientation with relevant methods and literature, but breadth across subfields and graduate training mechanics is compressed.

21_public_health.summary: One of the stronger health files, with concrete methods, datasets, and field-appropriate landmark literature; it still needs more explicit graduate milestones and course-by-course depth.

22_nursing.summary: Professionally grounded and reasonably concrete, but the file is short on literature depth, graduate structure, and level-aware text curation.

23_pharmacy.summary: Good professional framing and core texts, yet research designs, guidelines, and literature coverage are not expansive enough for a strong research-entry map.

24_dentistry.summary: Clinically realistic but relatively compressed, with thinner literature and methods or infrastructure detail than the quality bar expects.

25_veterinary_medicine.summary: Basic professional structure is present, but methods, literature, and research-depth treatment look sparse for a broad clinical and research field.

26_allied_health.summary: Useful high-level overview, but it is structurally one of the thinnest health files and lacks enough literature concreteness and research-infrastructure detail.

27_economics.summary: Strong social-science structure with solid methods and classic texts, but several literature entries are broad placeholders rather than exact must-read papers or books.

28_political_science.summary: Good theory and methods framing with a usable baseline structure, but graduate depth and canonical literature remain compact and not fully concrete.

29_psychology.summary: Reasonable field map and methods coverage, but literature depth and research-entry specificity appear modest relative to Requirement.md.

30_sociology.summary: Good methodological pluralism and subfield range, but many literature items are umbrella references rather than tightly specified canonical works.

31_anthropology.summary: Solid disciplinary framing, but methods, literature, and graduate depth remain only partially developed.

32_linguistics.summary: Good field fit and better-than-average methods coverage, but the research reading roadmap is still compact rather than deeply curated.

33_geography.summary: Useful cross-physical and human geography structure, yet the file is baseline rather than fully research-ready in literature and curricular detail.

34_communications.summary: Adequate overview, but communications needs more concrete methods traditions, subfield-specific canon, and graduate research structure.

35_history.summary: Strong humanities realism with concrete historiography and a substantial book and article canon; textbook mapping and graduate milestone detail are lighter than the top standard.

36_philosophy.summary: One of the strongest humanities files, with substantial canonical coverage and clear methodological traditions; it still needs more level-aware course-text mapping and research-training mechanics.

37_english_literature.summary: Good canon and thematic reading depth for a book-centered field, but curriculum and research progression are less fully built out than in the best files.

38_religious_studies.summary: Strong book-centered framing and credible methods for the discipline, though the research roadmap remains somewhat compact and less differentiated by subfield.

39_classics.summary: Good field realism around languages, texts, and history with a credible canon, but graduate milestones and subfield-specific research infrastructure could be more explicit.

40_fine_arts.summary: Appropriately practice-centered, but methods, workflows, and literature remain broad and lighter than needed for robust research or studio-readiness.

41_music.summary: Strong for an arts field, with a clear training model, concrete methods, and a substantial field-appropriate canon; research and practice pathways could still be more explicit.

42_theater.summary: Reasonably aligned to practice-centered theater training, but the file is compact and thin on methods, workflows, and landmark scholarship.

43_architecture.summary: Strong practice-field realism with studio, tools, licensure, and a serious canonical reading list; it is still lighter on explicit graduate milestones and the research-practice split.

44_design.summary: Good design-field fit and methods orientation, but literature and curriculum depth remain concise rather than full research-entry quality.

45_accounting.summary: Strong professional realism, concrete tools, and a credible mix of textbooks and standards, but graduate depth is effectively absent and the research roadmap is still partial.

46_finance.summary: Strong business-field structure with canonical papers and realistic methods and datasets; it remains somewhat thin on graduate milestones and broader research infrastructure.

47_marketing.summary: Good subfield framing and research topics for a business discipline, but literature depth and curriculum specificity are more compact than in the stronger files.

48_management.summary: Solid overview of management as a research field, though methods and literature remain somewhat generic relative to the requirement.

49_information_systems.summary: Reasonable field-specific structure and business-tech bridge, but the reading roadmap is concise and only partially concrete.

50_supply_chain_operations.summary: Good operations orientation and methods realism, but literature and level-aware curriculum depth appear serviceable rather than strong.

51_law.summary: One of the better professional-field files, with strong doctrinal structure, concrete canonical cases and books, and clear method realism; it still needs a fuller research-entry map beyond survey-level course texts.

52_education.summary: Strong education-field coverage with clear teacher-prep and research tracks plus a substantial canon; methods are good, but course-to-text coverage and research-readiness could still be tighter.

53_agricultural_sciences.summary: Useful disciplinary overview, but agriculture needs more concrete methods, datasets, experimental infrastructure, and landmark literature than this concise file provides.

54_forestry_natural_resources.summary: Reasonable field framing, yet research infrastructure, methods specificity, and canonical readings are too thin for a natural-resources roadmap.

55_cognitive_science.summary: Strong interdisciplinary framing with credible methods and a substantial cross-field reading list; it is still somewhat survey-like and uneven in level-aware textbook and course mapping.

56_neuroscience.summary: Good field fit and plausible frontier topics, but the file is relatively short for neuroscience and underdeveloped in methods, datasets, and canonical paper depth.

57_environmental_studies.summary: Useful interdisciplinary overview, but environmental studies needs more explicit method pluralism and literature concreteness to meet the repository standard.

58_gender_race_ethnic_studies.summary: Strong humanities and social-theory fit with a substantial, concrete canon and clear methods; it is lighter on graduate training mechanics and research infrastructure than the strongest files.

59_bioethics.summary: Good interdisciplinary and professional fit with credible canonical works and contemporary frontiers, but methods, guidelines or cases, and formal research pathways are still somewhat compact.

60_quantum_science.summary: Strong emerging-field roadmap with concrete tools, hardware, algorithms, and landmark papers; some literature remains review- or placeholder-oriented and the curricular path is still less standardized than mature disciplines.
